message:
[MED] fcp-telemetry: redact hyphenated header forms and AWS access_key

log_request_response's default redact list (expanded in 6706140b to add
bearer/cookie/credential/private_key/session) still missed two concrete
credential-leak paths that surfaced in a fresh-eyes audit of the
substring matcher:

1. Hyphenated header names. redact_sensitive is a case-insensitive
   substring matcher on JSON keys. When an HTTP request is serialized
   for logging, header names keep their hyphenated form — "X-API-Key",
   "X-Api-Key", "X-Private-Key". None of the existing substrings
   ("api_key", "private_key", ...) use hyphens, so header-form
   credentials were logged in the clear. Added "api-key", "access-key",
   and "private-key" alongside the underscore forms.

2. AWS-style access_key / access_key_id / aws_access_key_id. None of
   these contained any prior substring — "api_key" is not a substring
   of "access_key_id", and "key" alone is too broad to add safely.
   Added "access_key" (and the hyphenated form above).

redact_sensitive's behavior (redact_sensitive_with_depth in
logging.rs:63) is unchanged. Adding substrings to the list is
backward-compatible: worst case a value that was previously logged
becomes "[REDACTED]". The expanded list stays well short of generic
substrings like "auth" or "key" that would false-positive on benign
fields (author, pubkey, etc.).

Regression test test_redact_catches_hyphenated_and_access_key_variants
pins X-API-Key, X-Api-Key, X-Private-Key, aws_access_key_id, and
access_key as redacted, while asserting user_name passes through
untouched to guard against future over-broad additions.

Surfaced-not-patched findings (in-flight, not shipped this pass):

  [LOW] fcp-sandbox/wasi.rs prepare_preopened_dir has a double-
  canonicalize TOCTOU: ensure_preopen_path_is_stable (wasi.rs:1283)
  canonicalizes once via resolve_policy_path; a subsequent
  path.canonicalize() at wasi.rs:1279 runs as a separate syscall.
  An attacker with local write access to an ancestor directory can
  swap a regular dir for a symlink between the two calls; the second
  canonicalize resolves through the attacker-chosen target while the
  earlier stability check saw the unhooked path. Fix: return the
  already-canonical path from ensure_preopen_path_is_stable and reuse
  it, eliminating the second syscall.

  [LOW] fcp-streaming/reconnect.rs delay_for_attempt still panics for
  max_delay near Duration::MAX due to f64 round-trip precision. The
  d84a286b clamp handles NaN and negative but not the case where
  max_delay.as_secs_f64() rounds up slightly past Duration::MAX,
  causing Duration::from_secs_f64 to panic on the clamped result.
  Pathological config only; ship behind a `try_from_secs_f64`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -158,14 +158,24 @@ pub fn log_request_response(
     let redact_fields = vec![
         "password".to_string(),
         "api_key".to_string(),
+        // Hyphenated form: JSON maps surfaced from HTTP headers preserve
+        // "X-API-Key" / "X-Api-Key" as-is — underscore-only substrings
+        // silently miss them.
+        "api-key".to_string(),
         "apikey".to_string(),
+        // AWS-style: access_key / access_key_id / aws_access_key_id never
+        // contained any of the prior substrings and were being logged raw.
+        "access_key".to_string(),
+        "access-key".to_string(),
         "secret".to_string(),
         "token".to_string(),
         "authorization".to_string(),
         "bearer".to_string(),
         "cookie".to_string(),
         "credential".to_string(),
         "private_key".to_string(),
+        // Hyphenated form for headers such as "X-Private-Key".
+        "private-key".to_string(),
         "session".to_string(),
     ];
 
@@ -981,4 +991,52 @@ mod tests {
         // Non-sensitive field passes through.
         assert_eq!(redacted["user_name"], "alice", "plain user_name untouched");
     }
+
+    /// Regression: JSON surfaced from HTTP headers keeps the hyphenated
+    /// form (e.g. `X-API-Key`). An underscore-only substring list
+    /// (`api_key`) missed the header form entirely, so `X-API-Key: secret`
+    /// was being logged in the clear. Also verify AWS-style
+    /// `aws_access_key_id` is now caught. The `user_name` field must
+    /// still pass through to prove the additions didn't go over-broad.
+    #[test]
+    fn test_redact_catches_hyphenated_and_access_key_variants() {
+        let expanded = vec![
+            "password".to_string(),
+            "api_key".to_string(),
+            "api-key".to_string(),
+            "apikey".to_string(),
+            "access_key".to_string(),
+            "access-key".to_string(),
+            "secret".to_string(),
+            "token".to_string(),
+            "authorization".to_string(),
+            "bearer".to_string(),
+            "cookie".to_string(),
+            "credential".to_string(),
+            "private_key".to_string(),
+            "private-key".to_string(),
+            "session".to_string(),
+        ];
+
+        let value = json!({
+            "X-API-Key": "k_hyphenated",
+            "X-Api-Key": "k_hyphenated_mixed",
+            "aws_access_key_id": "AKIA_example_value",
+            "access_key": "raw_key",
+            "X-Private-Key": "-----BEGIN_example",
+            "user_name": "alice",
+        });
+
+        let redacted = redact_sensitive(&value, &expanded);
+
+        assert_eq!(redacted["X-API-Key"], "[REDACTED]", "X-API-Key");
+        assert_eq!(redacted["X-Api-Key"], "[REDACTED]", "X-Api-Key");
+        assert_eq!(
+            redacted["aws_access_key_id"], "[REDACTED]",
+            "aws_access_key_id"
+        );
+        assert_eq!(redacted["access_key"], "[REDACTED]", "access_key");
+        assert_eq!(redacted["X-Private-Key"], "[REDACTED]", "X-Private-Key");
+        assert_eq!(redacted["user_name"], "alice", "user_name must pass");
+    }
 }
